title: ChatGPT closes 50-year math conjecture as Detroit cuts deepen - TCR 05/16/26
description:
ChatGPT solved Erdős Problem 1196 in 80 minutes after a Stanford mathematician spent seven years on it, a Cell paper documented spinach chloroplasts producing energy inside living mouse retinas for several days, and Detroit's three legacy automakers have cut more than 20,000 salaried jobs since 2022 with Ford's CEO saying AI will absorb half the remaining white-collar workforce.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-16-2026/

0:00 ChatGPT closes Erdős Problem 1196
1:55 A Fifty-Year-Old Problem In Eighty Minutes
4:35 Spinach chloroplasts power mouse retinas
7:27 Detroit's 20,000 salaried job cuts
11:07 Judge pauses Anthropic copyright settlement
14:14 Close

This episode covers the Erdős 1196 proof and the compression of mathematical research time, the spinach-chloroplast cross-kingdom transplant in mouse retinas, vitamin B2 as the ferroptosis-evasion cofactor with roseoflavin as candidate molecule, NASA's radiation-hardened neuromorphic chip running on-orbit inference 500x faster than current spaceflight computers, the ensitrelvir Phase III household-prophylaxis trial cutting symptomatic COVID from 9 to 2.9 percent, and Judge Martinez-Olguin pausing Anthropic's $1.5 billion author settlement over the $320 million fee versus $3,000 per-book ratio.

Mark Sellke had worked on Erdős Problem 1196 intermittently for seven years. ChatGPT with a code interpreter returned a structural proof in roughly 80 minutes, and the proof checked out. The conjecture-and-test loop that bounded mathematics for centuries just compressed into a single session, with hundreds of open Erdős problems sitting in the same register. The Cell paper crossing the plant-animal kingdom boundary inside a mouse eye moves the date when retinal dystrophy becomes a real conversation, and reshapes a textbook assumption that has organized biology since Aristotle. The Würzburg team naming vitamin B2 as the FSP1 cofactor cancer cells exploit, with roseoflavin already triggering ferroptotic death across multiple tumor lines, adds another locatable metabolic crutch to the catalog cancer biology has been building over the past five years. NASA's neuromorphic processor running radiation-hardened inference at 500x current throughput closes the loop on satellite autonomy. The ensitrelvir trial extends antiviral use from active infection to post-exposure prophylaxis. And the judge declining to sign off on the largest AI copyright settlement to date puts the arithmetic that made the settlement architecture work on the public docket for the first time.
